title: The lock-key composition with bolder proportions. The state block is larger and fills more of the frame; the arrow void is wider so it reads at small scale; the governing operator key is positioned immediately adjacent to the block's right edge. Together they show that the transition happens when the operator exactly fits the state's declared interface — no other key works.
conceptualBridge: The lock-key arrow means Precept's transition requires exact structural alignment because the state's interface is the exact shape of the governing operator — nothing else is declared, so nothing else can fit.
decision: Shortlisted: 4.63 avg — the bolder proportions resolve the legibility issue from 60-lock-key; the two-piece lock-and-arrow-key composition has the highest conceptual bridge score in the set (4.5) and a perfect structural soundness score (5); the distinct silhouette is memorable and specific to Precept's state-event-transition model.
